learningObjective: Understand that approaching from left vs right can yield different limit values
ahaMoment: Toggling direction shows the limit changes from 1 to 3, proving the two-sided limit doesn't exist

miniSpec.title: Direction Toggle
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: Tracer point switches sides, limit readout changes value
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: Toggle direction → tracer moves to that side → limit value updates → 1 vs 3 revealed
miniSpec.aha_moment: Left limit is 1, right limit is 3 — they differ, so no two-sided limit exists

modelVariables.leftLimitValue: derived: the limit value when approaching from left (always 1)
modelVariables.rightLimitValue: derived: the limit value when approaching from right (always 3)

assertions.description: On load, direction is 'left' showing one limit, inviting toggle to discover the difference
assertions.visualCheck: Tracer on left side of x=0, amber colored, approaching y=1

assertions.description: Toggling to 'right' shows a different limit value
assertions.visualCheck: Tracer jumps to right side, coral colored, limit readout shows 3 instead of 1

assertions.description: Toggling back to 'left' returns to the original limit
assertions.visualCheck: Tracer back on left side, amber colored, limit readout shows 1
